name: Tasks View
description:
VS Code WebviewView sidebar panel showing the Code Workbench task board, plus the full editor-tab Task Board page and the task-bound phase flow. Hosts the shared @code-workbench/ui TasksPanel React component in a webview and answers its list/create/update/remove/openInEditor/startPhase RPC calls via the shared task-store. The RPC handler set and context payload are exported (buildTaskRpcHandlers, taskPanelContext) and shared with the full editor-tab Task Board page (tasksPage.ts, command codeWorkbench.tasks.openAsPage). The page pushes surface:'page' in its context, switching TasksPanel into pageMode: clicking a task opens an inline TaskDetailPane editor (title/description/memo/priority/status/worktree/epic/tags + subtasks; the backing .md file open is a secondary button), and a toolbar adds group-by (worktree/epic/flat), status filter (active/all/open/in-progress/done) and tag filter. Sidebar keeps the compact accordion behavior. The tasks file watcher (watchTasks) refreshes both surfaces.

Task-bound phase flow (commands/taskFlow.ts): a root task carries an optional `phase` field ('plan'|'implement'|'review'|'fix'|null). TaskDetailPane renders a PhaseStepper (only for root tasks, when the host provides TasksApi.startPhase) showing done/active/upcoming pills plus one "Start <next phase> →" button. Clicking it calls startPhase RPC → buildTaskRpcHandlers delegates to the registered command codeWorkbench.tasks.startPhase(taskId, phase), which resolves the task, sets its phase via updateTask, resolves its worktree (task.worktree is a worktreeKey — lowercased basename, NOT a path — resolved against listWorktrees(), falling back to the active worktree for unassigned tasks), and spawns a Claude session via SessionManager.create with a phase-specific model/prompt: Plan (opus, --permission-mode plan, explores + files 'plan-step' subtasks + writes memo), Implement (sonnet, works plan-step subtasks), Review (sonnet, reviews the diff + files 'review-finding' subtasks), Fix (sonnet, works review-finding subtasks). Each phase's prompt tells the spawned session to task_update the task's `phase` field when handing off — the board is the state machine, not the chat transcript. Subtasks can't nest, so the stepper and phase field only apply to root tasks. The MCP tasks-server.mjs exposes `phase` on task_create/task_update (validated enum) and renders it as `<phase>` in task_list output (root tasks only, alongside {epic}).

Supporting session-manager changes: SavedSession/CreateSessionOptions gained permissionMode (→ `claude --permission-mode`, mutually exclusive with yolo args) and effortOverride (takes precedence over the worktree's effort pref) so a Plan-phase session can force read-only planning and its own effort level.
guidelines: All task file I/O goes through the shared task-store — never implement parallel task logic in the extension.
Watch task files with watchTasks() and call tasksProvider.refresh() on change; refresh() posts a tasks-changed event to the webview.
The panel UI is the shared ui TasksPanel — render it via a React webview, do not hand-write panel markup.
Task formatting/parsing belongs in mcp-core task-store, not the extension — tasks.ts is a thin re-export.
Page-only UI (detail editor, filter toolbar) is gated on the pageMode prop, driven by surface:'page' in the context event — the same React bundle serves both surfaces; never fork the bundle.
anti_patterns: Do not implement task CRUD directly in the extension — delegate to the shared task-store.
Do not rebuild the task panel as vanilla-JS webview HTML — use the shared ui React component.
Do not reintroduce a local taskFormat module — formatting lives in the shared task-store.
Do not make opening the .md file the primary click action on the page — the inline TaskDetailPane is the editor; the file open is secondary.
decisions: The tasks module is a thin re-export of the shared task-store.
The panel was migrated from a vanilla-JS webview script to a React webview rendering the shared ui TasksPanel; VS Code-level filter/show-done commands are kept as compatibility stubs.
Task formatting/parsing was consolidated into the shared task-store (mcp-core); the extension's taskFormat.ts was deleted to avoid divergence.
Page mode reuses the row-click onOpenTask hook internally for selection instead of adding a parallel selection API.
